sizing_rule.note: Position quantity scales with current account equity level. Sizing analysis uses HPR (Holding Period Return) metrics—specifically AHPR (arithmetic mean HPR) and population standard deviation of HPRs—to optimize the tradeoff between average trade return and volatility. Author (Ralph Vince) remains silent on specific optimal_f value, kelly_fraction, and max_drawdown_tolerance thresholds; these must be derived from historical trade data.

source_rule_text: Position sizing should be determined as a function of account equity level, using HPRs (Holding Period Returns) expressed as percentages rather than dollar amounts to enable mathematical precision in money management strategies.
